name: First Classic Brownies
recipeIngredient: 8 tbsp Unsalted butter (margarine or shortening), at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 250°F.
2. Chop the chocolate and place it in an ovenproof pan. Melt in the oven, stirring occasionally, until smooth.
3. Remove the melted chocolate from the oven. Increase the oven temperature to 325°F.
4. Grease a 9-inch square baking pan.
5. In a bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt.
6. With an electric mixer on medium-low speed, beat butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
7. Beat in eggs one at a time.
8. Slowly beat in the melted chocolate, then vanilla extract.
9. Reduce the speed to low and add the flour mixture; beat until just combined.
10. Stir in chopped walnuts (if using).
11. Spread the batter into the prepared pan.
12. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until tested done with a toothpick.
13. Allow the brownies to cool before cutting into squares.
Reminder: If using a glass baking pan, reduce the oven heat by 25°F.
